body.title: CLOSED: Semantic palace retrieval in /context-load Step 2b
body.result: added Step 2c conditional in-flight semantic search to /context-load
body.description:
Context-load Step 2b loads the palace tree by structural hash-gating. When there is an in-flight task on the slate, a semantic search against that task would pull the most relevant palace nodes without loading the full tree — targeted context at session start.

Proposed shape: after Step 2b palace tree hash check, if ## In-flight on slate is not NONE, extract the in-flight task description and call mcp__igor__memory_search(query=<in_flight_text>, limit=5). Inject results as 'Relevant palace nodes for in-flight task:' block. When in-flight is NONE, skip (no meaningful query). Additive — doesn't replace structural tree listing.

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/context-load (Step 2b addition)
**Design rules:** none
**Scope boundary:** one new conditional step after Step 2b. Only fires when in-flight task exists. No state changes.
**Test plan:** run /context-load with in-flight set to a specific ticket; verify palace nodes relevant to that ticket appear without being in the standard structural load.
**Pass condition:** /context-load with in-flight 'T-cc-log-stop-hook-wire' surfaces palace nodes about agent_datacenter logging architecture.
